title: Multiple Granularity Locking Hierarchy
description: A hierarchical diagram showing the levels of data granularity and the corresponding lock modes (IS, IX, S, SIX, X) that can be applied at each level to implement multiple granularity locking in a DBMS.
relationships: Hierarchical containment from largest (Database) to smallest (Record) granularity
Lock modes are requested at a specific level and implicitly lock all descendants of that node in the hierarchy
